category_title: Elementary students with ASD experiencing emotional outbursts and academic refusal during family transitions
minimal_context: Gabriel is sitting in his general education classroom and is asked to begin working on a modified academic assignment.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Gabriel, here is your modified assignment for today. Please get started.

STUDENT: I like to come to my school, Apple Elementary. I like the adults at my school, like Mrs. Jones, Mr. Bell, and Mrs. Scott.

TUTOR: That is nice. Now please look at your paper and begin.

STUDENT: There are lots of things I like at school. (Does not look at the assignment)

TUTOR: You need to participate and do your work right now.

STUDENT: (Pushes the paper away and begins to have an emotional outburst) No! I am refusing to participate in this assignment!

options.A: Terminate demands and remove the assignment materials for 10 seconds contingent upon the outburst.
options.B: Give the student a warning at the time but do not discipline him further.
options.C: Teach the student the technique of 'stepping back' and not acting on the 'first thought'.
options.D: Read a social story together with the student and include a comprehension check.
